problem.title: Reverse Words in a String
problem.description:
Given an input string s, reverse the order of the words.
                
Note: A word is defined as a sequence of non-space characters. The words in s will be separated by at least one space.
assumptions: No leading or trailing spaces in output.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Using Built-in Functions
solutions.description: The simplest approach is to split the string by spaces, reverse the array, and join it back.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Two Pointers (In-place for char array)
solutions.description: If we're given a char array and need to do it in-place:
solutions.code_python:
from typing import List

def reverseWords_inplace(chars: List[str]) -> None:
    """Reverse words in a char list in-place (e.g. chars = list(s))."""
    def reverse(l: int, r: int) -> None:
        while l < r:
            chars[l], chars[r] = chars[r], chars[l]
            l += 1
            r -= 1

    # Step 1: reverse entire array
    reverse(0, len(chars) - 1)

    # Step 2: reverse each word
    start = 0
    for i in range(len(chars)):
        if chars[i] == ' ':
            reverse(start, i - 1)
            start = i + 1

    # Step 3: reverse last word
    reverse(start, len(chars) - 1)
